title: satureja obovata subsp. canescens
intro: Satureja obovata subsp. canescens, also known as mountain savory, is a subspecies of the satureja obovata plant, a member of the Lamiaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, with a strong, pungent flavor and aroma similar to that of oregano. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and respiratory infections. Mountain savory can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or dried herb, and has been used to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and coughs, while also providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits.
